title: Salesforce, Inc. (CRM) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: A comprehensive two-stage DCF valuation of Salesforce (CRM), estimating its intrinsic value based on forward cash flow projections, cost of capital, and terminal growth. Economic Prospect Score: 71/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Salesforce's revenue growth has naturally decelerated given its massive scale, dropping from the 20%+ levels of the past decade down to the low double-digits. However, aggressive restructuring and a renewed focus on operating leverage have supercharged Free Cash Flow. A 12% FCF growth rate assumes continued margin expansion, steady subscription retention, and incremental monetization of AI features (Einstein Copilot) offsetting macroeconomic headwinds.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: behind my chosen inputs for Salesforce.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: behind my chosen inputs for Salesforce.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

items.title: AI Disruption vs. Adoption
items.description: While Salesforce is integrating AI (Einstein), there is a risk that smaller, more agile AI-native startups could disrupt CRM's moat, leading to higher churn and depressed growth rates.

items.title: Macroeconomic Sensitivity
items.description: As enterprise software budgets tighten during economic downturns, seat expansions stall, which could result in FCF growth severely underperforming the 12% baseline.

items.title: Capital Allocation
items.description: CRM has a history of aggressive M&A (Slack, Tableau). If management pivots away from capital return (buybacks) back toward expensive acquisitions, FCF yields could suffer.

faq.question: Is Salesforce (CRM) stock overvalued or undervalued?
faq.answer: Based on our DCF model, Salesforce is estimated to have an intrinsic value of $376.78. Compared to its current price of $193.99, the stock appears undervalued.

faq.question: What is a two-stage DCF model?
faq.answer: A two-stage Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model estimates a company's value by forecasting free cash flows over an initial high-growth period (e.g., 5 years), and then applying a slower, perpetual terminal growth rate for all remaining years. These future cash flows are then discounted back to their present value.

faq.question: Why use a 9.5% discount rate for Salesforce?
faq.answer: A 9.5% Weighted Average Cost of Capital (WACC) reflects the risks associated with investing in a mature tech giant. It accounts for a risk-free rate of 4.18%, an equity risk premium, and a beta of 1.307, balanced by the company's cost of debt.

faq.question: Why is Free Cash Flow used instead of Net Income?
faq.answer: Free Cash Flow represents the actual cash a company generates after accounting for capital expenditures required to maintain or expand its asset base. It is a purer measure of profitability than Net Income, which includes non-cash accounting expenses like depreciation.

prospectVerdictDetail: Salesforce has successfully transitioned from growth-at-all-costs to profitable growth under activist investor pressure. Margins have expanded dramatically. Agentforce (AI agents) is the next growth vector. The CRM market is mature but Salesforce's platform breadth creates cross-sell opportunities.
prospectKeyRisks: AI copilots from Microsoft (Copilot) and Google reducing Salesforce's differentiation in CRM productivity
Enterprise software spending rationalization as companies consolidate vendors and reduce seat counts
Agentforce adoption slower than marketed — enterprise AI agent deployment requires significant customization
prospectKeyCatalysts: Agentforce driving a new consumption-based pricing model that expands Salesforce's revenue per customer
Data Cloud and AI capabilities increasing platform stickiness and enabling premium pricing
Continued margin expansion to 35%+ operating margins as the business matures and growth investments moderate
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
